title: Find All Pairs with Given Sum

statement.markdown:
Given two integer arrays `arr1` and `arr2`, write a function that returns all pairs of integers `(x, y)` where `x` is from `arr1` and `y` is from `arr2` such that `x + y` equals a given target `sum`. The pairs should be returned in sorted order, and each pair should only appear once.

Example: If `arr1 = [1, 2, 3]` and `arr2 = [4, 5, 6]` and the target sum is `7`, then the output should be `[[1, 6], [2, 5], [3, 4]]`.
statement.inputFormat: Two sorted integer arrays `arr1` and `arr2`, and a target sum `sum`.
statement.outputFormat: A 2D array of pairs, where each pair is an array of two integers.
statement.notes: The input arrays are not empty, and the target sum is within the range of possible sums.

hints.text: Use two pointers to traverse the arrays

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to use two pointers, one for each array, to traverse the arrays and find pairs that add up to the target sum.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to generate all possible pairs and check if they add up to the target sum.
editorial.betterSolution: Using two pointers, we can efficiently find all pairs that add up to the target sum.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use two pointers, one for each array, to traverse the arrays and find pairs that add up to the target sum.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proved by showing that all pairs that add up to the target sum are found, and each pair is only counted once.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run is as follows: (1) Initialize two pointers, one for each array. (2) If the current sum of the two elements at the pointers is less than the target sum, move the pointer of the array with the smaller element. (3) If the current sum of the two elements at the pointers is greater than the target sum, move the pointer of the array with the larger element. (4) If the current sum of the two elements at the pointers is equal to the target sum, add the pair to the result and move both pointers.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void getPairs(std::vector<int>& arr1, std::vector<int>& arr2, int sum) { std::vector<int> res; for (int i = 0; i < arr1.size(); i++) { for (int j = 0; j < arr2.size(); j++) { if (arr1[i] + arr2[j] == sum) { res.push_back(arr1[i]); res.push_back(arr2[j]); } } } } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public void getPairs(int[] arr1, int[] arr2, int sum) { java.util.ArrayList<Integer> res = new java.util.ArrayList<>(); for (int i = 0; i < arr1.length; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < arr2.length; j++) { if (arr1[i] + arr2[j] == sum) { res.add(arr1[i]); res.add(arr2[j]); } } } } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def get_pairs(self, arr1, arr2, sum): res = [] for i in range(len(arr1)): for j in range(len(arr2)): if arr1[i] + arr2[j] == sum: res.append(arr1[i]); res.append(arr2[j]); return res
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function getPairs(arr1, arr2, sum) { var res = []; for (var i = 0; i < arr1.length; i++) { for (var j = 0; j < arr2.length; j++) { if (arr1[i] + arr2[j] == sum) { res.push(arr1[i]); res.push(arr2[j]); } } } return res; }
